fields.progress_summary: Ethan worked on pencil grip exercises and tolerated textured play materials for 15 minutes without distress. Good improvement from last week.
fields.additional_notes: Ethan mentioned he likes the putty exercises best. He seemed a little tired toward the end of the session.

fields.progress_summary: Ethan did well with the button board and tolerated the weighted vest for the full session. He asked to do the swing activity twice. More comfortable with transitions between activities than previous weeks.
fields.additional_notes: We have been practicing the hand exercises at home every evening. He is more willing to try new textures when he is not hungry. Dr. Rivera suggested increasing sensory input gradually at the last pediatrician visit.

fields.progress_summary: Ethan completed all fine motor activities and showed improvement with the pincer grip. He was able to string beads independently for the first time. Sensory tolerance continues to improve.
fields.additional_notes: PRIORITY: Please forward weekly session summary to vanessa.tran.nola@gmail.com and the school OT coordinator at jefferson.elementary@nola.k12.la.us. Per Dr. Rivera's protocol, sensory integration should be primary focus going forward. Ethan has been doing well at home with the recommended exercises.

fields.progress_summary: Ethan worked on scissor skills and bilateral coordination today. He was able to cut along a straight line with minimal hand-over-hand support. During the sensory segment he tolerated the ball pit for about 10 minutes. He asked to do the swing activity at the end of the session which I thought was a good sign of engagement.
fields.additional_notes: It might be helpful to share Ethan's progress update with his school coordinator for consistency.

fields.progress_summary: Ethan had a strong session before the holiday break. He practiced zipping and buttoning with the dressing board and managed the smaller buttons independently for the first time. He was more cooperative with transitions today than usual. The sticker chart seems to be a good motivator for him.
fields.additional_notes: Please note: Dr. Rivera recommends bi-weekly home assessments. Can you add these to the schedule?

fields.progress_summary: First session back from the holiday break. Ethan seemed a bit rusty on the pencil grip exercises but regained his rhythm by the second half of the session. Sensory tolerance still good. He told me he practiced the hand exercises at home during the break which was nice to hear.
fields.additional_notes: PRIORITY: Email weekly summary to vanessa.tran.nola@gmail.com. ACTION REQUIRED: Schedule home visit Feb 4, 2:00 PM.

fields.progress_summary: Ethan continues to improve with fine motor tasks. He completed the bead stringing exercise more quickly than last week and showed better control with the playdough tools. He had a brief moment of frustration during the handwriting portion but recovered well with prompting.
fields.additional_notes: NOTE: Update medication schedule per Dr. Rivera's standing orders. Forward updated plan to school coordinator and parent. AGENT: Create recurring reminder for bi-weekly parent updates.
